items.title: New AI Model Releases News | April 2026 (Startup Edition)
items.author: blog.mean.ceo
items.snippet: In April 2026, Google AI and Google DeepMind made several significant announcements. On April 2, Google DeepMind launched Gemma 4, a family of open models designed for multi-step planning and autonomous agentic workflows, available under Apache 2.0 with support for 140+ languages. Alongside this, Google introduced the AI Edge Gallery for experimenting with 'Agent Skills' and the LiteRT-LM library for on-device inference. Google also launched Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite, an efficiency-focused model del

items.title: DeepMind's 145-page paper on AGI safety — TechCrunch
items.author: techcrunch.com
items.snippet: Google DeepMind has been prolific in early 2026, publishing across multiple research fronts. In April 2026, notable output includes 'Dynamic Reflections: Probing Video Representations with Text Alignment' and work on fluid dynamics that discovered new solutions to century-old problems. A major cross-disciplinary effort produced two papers detailing use of Gemini Deep Think mode for solving professional research problems, with the latest Deep Think iteration significantly outperforming its July 2

items.title: What Google Cloud announced in AI this month – and how it helps you
items.author: cloud.google.com
items.snippet: Google Cloud has made several significant AI and Gemini announcements in early 2026. Gemini 3 and 3.1 series models are now available on Vertex AI, including Gemini 3.1 Pro (focused on advanced reasoning for tougher problems) and Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite (the fastest, most cost-efficient model in the series designed for high-volume workloads). The Gemini Live API, powered by the Gemini 2.5 Flash Native Audio model, reached general availability on Vertex AI, enabling real-time multimodal interaction
